title: AI Image Generation Request Flow
interaction_flow: Client → DriverService: call({ iface: 'puter-image-generation', driver: 'openai-image-generation', method: 'generate', args: { prompt, model?, provider?, ratio?, quality? } })
DriverService → DriverService: _call() — _process_args(iface, method, args) [validates & consolidates arg types from registry]
DriverService → DriverService: resolve driver name (iface_to_driver map: 'puter-image-generation' → 'openai-image-generation' by default)
DriverService → DriverService: resolve service aliases (interface_service_aliases / service_aliases lookup)
DriverService → DriverService: get_service_or_throw_(driverName, iface) → returns AIImageGenerationService
DriverService → EventService: emit('driver.create-call-context', event) [sets up client_driver_call context with intended_service, test_mode, response_metadata]
DriverService → PermissionService: scan(actor, 'service:<driver>:ii:<iface>') [checks permission options]
PermissionService → DriverService: returns reading/options
DriverService → DriverService: select_best_option_(options) → get_policies_for_option_(option) [resolves effective policy, rate-limit]
DriverService → DriverService: Invoker.create with decorators: [enforce-rate-limit → svc_rateLimit.check_and_increment(), add-metadata, result-coercion via svc_coercion.coerce()]
DriverService → AIImageGenerationService: service.as('puter-image-generation').generate({ prompt, model, provider, ratio, quality, test_mode })
AIImageGenerationService → AIImageGenerationService: Context.get('client_driver_call') — extract test_mode, intended_service (legacyProviderName)

AIImageGenerationService → AIImageGenerationService: resolve intendedProvider (parameters.provider || legacyProviderName fallback → DEFAULT_PROVIDER 'openai-image-generation' || configuredProviders[0])
AIImageGenerationService → AIImageGenerationService: if no model → provider.getDefaultModel()
AIImageGenerationService → AIImageGenerationService: getModel({ modelId: parameters.model, provider: intendedProvider }) → looks up #modelIdMap, prefers exact puterId match
AIImageGenerationService → AIImageGenerationService: validate/clamp ratio against model.allowedRatios (default 1024x1024), validate quality against model.allowedQualityLevels
AIImageGenerationService → IImageProvider: provider.generate({ prompt, model: model.id, provider: model.provider, ratio, quality, test_mode })
IImageProvider (OpenAI) → IImageProvider: if test_mode → return 'https://puter-sample-data.puter.site/image_example.png'
IImageProvider (OpenAI) → IImageProvider: #estimatePromptTokenCount(prompt) + #buildPriceKey(model, quality, size)
IImageProvider (OpenAI) → MeteringService: hasEnoughCredits(actor, estimatedTotalCostInMicroCents)
MeteringService → IImageProvider: returns boolean (throws APIError 'insufficient_funds' if false)
IImageProvider (OpenAI) → ExternalImageAPI: openai.images.generate({ model, prompt, size, quality, user })
ExternalImageAPI → IImageProvider: returns ImagesResponse { data[0].url | data[0].b64_json, usage? }
IImageProvider (OpenAI) → IImageProvider: #extractUsage(result) → compute input/output costs in cents
IImageProvider (OpenAI) → MeteringService: batchIncrementUsages(actor, [{ usageType, usageAmount, costOverride }])
IImageProvider (OpenAI) → IImageProvider: #setResponseCostMetadata → writes to Context client_driver_call.response_metadata
IImageProvider → AIImageGenerationService: returns url (string: 'https://...' or 'data:image/...')
AIImageGenerationService → AIImageGenerationService: wrap url in TypedValue({ $: 'string:url:web'|'string:url:data', content_type: 'image' }, url)
AIImageGenerationService → DriverService: returns TypedValue
DriverService (result-coercion) → DriverService: svc_coercion.coerce(desired_type, TypedValue) → coerced value
DriverService (add-metadata) → DriverService: wraps result as { success: true, service: { name, version }, result, metadata: response_metadata }
DriverService → Client: returns { success: true, service, result: <image url>, metadata: { cost, cost_components } }
key_relationships: AIImageGenerationService IMPLEMENTS 'puter-image-generation' driver interface (generate method) and 'driver-capabilities' (supports_test_mode)
AIImageGenerationService depends_on DriverService (registers service aliases per provider at boot)
AIImageGenerationService depends_on EventService (emit 'ai.image.registerProviders' for extension providers)
AIImageGenerationService composes IImageProvider[] (OpenAi, Gemini, Together, Cloudflare, Replicate, XAI) — registered in #providers map
DriverService orchestrates call() → _call() → call_new_() pipeline with permission check, rate-limiting, coercion
IImageProvider (all) depends_on MeteringService (credits check + usage recording)
Model resolution: #modelIdMap[modelId] → IImageModel[] sorted by cost; supports aliases and puterId
boot_time_registration: For each provider: driverService.register_service_alias('ai-image', providerName, { iface: 'puter-image-generation' }) — all legacy provider names route to AIImageGenerationService
For each provider: provider.models() → build #modelIdMap (id + puterId aliases), sort by cost
error_cases: No providers configured → throws Error('no image generation providers configured')
Model not found → APIError 'field_invalid' with available models URL
Insufficient credits → APIError 'insufficient_funds' (thrown inside provider before API call)
Rate limit exceeded → thrown by svc_rateLimit.check_and_increment() inside Invoker decorator
important_notes: DEFAULT_PROVIDER = 'openai-image-generation'. Default model = 'dall-e-2'. Ratio default = 1024x1024. test_mode short-circuits to static sample image URL. Cost metadata is written to Context (client_driver_call.response_metadata) and returned in response.metadata. Metering uses microCents (cents * 1_000_000). Provider resolution: parameters.provider → legacyProviderName (intended_service from DriverService context) → DEFAULT_PROVIDER → configuredProviders[0].
